programmes.show_description: Killer: With Tara on holiday, Steed enlists his aristocratic friend to help him find the assassin responsible for the deaths of multiple agents. The case leads them to an unlikely killer.
Mail Call... Again: Colonel Potter gets great news: he's going to become a grandpa. Frank gets bad news: he's going to be an ex-husband. His wife reveals that she knows all about Margaret.
The Price of Tomato Juice: Radar learns that Potter likes tomato juice and tries to procure some for him. Hawkeye and BJ warn him that a request will quickly result in a complicated ordeal.
Dear Ma: Radar writes to his mother with details of the foot inspection at the 4077th. Potter is shot in the backside but refuses anaesthetic so he can watch Hawkeye operate.
Open House: Henry returns home early from a business trip to discover that Matthew has thrown a house party without his permission.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Song Of The Wild West: Jonathan and Mark visit a country-western bar where they meet a singer hoping to follow in her mother's footsteps. A broken family also needs Jonathan's assistance.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 1: Jonathan steps in to help Deke, a resentful young athlete whose legs are amputated below the knee following a tragic motorcycle accident.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Choices: Jonathan and Mark become private investigators for their assignment. Their clients are a Vietnamese couple trying to track down the sons they were forced to give up during the war.
Summer Camp: After suffering major burns in a freak accident, a fashion model and actress becomes a counsellor at a summer camp for blind children run by Jonathan and Mark's friend Frank.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Four Eyes: The mysterious decline in Mary's school performance is explained when Charles discovers that she needs glasses - but Mary loses her confidence when her class begins to tease her.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Ebenezer Sprague: Laura Ingalls is stunned to learn that her friendly fishing friend is Walnut Grove's new banker and is upset when he accuses her of befriending him to secure a loan.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: In the Big Inning: Walnut Grove's baseball team finally gets the chance to beat its main rival, but the town's lack of confidence in the team stirs up trouble for Slick McBurney.
The Inferno: John-Boy is deeply affected when he witnesses the crash of the Hindenburg. Meanwhile, Curt tries to find a way to be alone with Mary Ellen.
The Heartbreaker: Jason Walton learns that love can be one of life's most painful experiences.
It's a Man's World: Niamh leaves Ambrose holding the baby, and Kathleen gets an unexpected visitor. Donal and Liam try to earn some extra cash.
The Doctor Now Departing: Tensions rise between Tom and Arthur over a possible move to New Zealand. Meanwhile, Eddie is referred to hospital with a suspicious lump.
Eleanor opens the charitable Thrift Infirmary and discovers that distinguishing between the deserving and undeserving poor is not only difficult but dangerous.

programmes.show_name: New: Good News on GREAT! Extra
programmes.show_description: An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Show Folk: Rumpole travels to the north of England to defend an actress in a murder trial.
Smilin' Jack: A helicopter pilot wants to get back to fighting despite his failing health while a soldier who received two Purple Hearts is tired of the bloodbath and wishes to be sent home.
The More I See You: A woman Hawkeye used to live with is assigned to the 4077th. Hawkeye admits it's not possible to simply work with her and they begin a passionate affair.
Deluge: Just as everyone is ready to relax the PA announces incoming wounded. The Chinese Army joins the war, killing any hopes the 4077th has about a speedy resolution and homecoming.
Jacko further complicates his life by getting involved with a novice nun, the new office secretary and a mystery girl at a tennis club.
Jacko is in a pickle; he's taken out the boss's daughter and left her stranded when he found out who she was. If Lionel finds out, he'll kill him - if Lesley finds out, she'll kill him.
As Lesley discovers Jacko's identity, she offers Lionel an ultimatum. Meanwhile, Jacko's duties as an uncle get in the way of his dating schedule. Who will come out on top?
Obsession: The team are tasked with investigating the whereabouts of a stolen rocket from a munitions warehouse. Meanwhile, Purdey experiences a tense encounter with her ex-boyfriend.
The Rotters: Tara and Steed investigate when members of the Institute of Timber Technology are killed. Will The Avengers locate the killers before they have the chance to execute their plan?
Smilin' Jack: A helicopter pilot wants to get back to fighting despite his failing health while a soldier who received two Purple Hearts is tired of the bloodbath and wishes to be sent home.
The More I See You: A woman Hawkeye used to live with is assigned to the 4077th. Hawkeye admits it's not possible to simply work with her and they begin a passionate affair.
Deluge: Just as everyone is ready to relax the PA announces incoming wounded. The Chinese Army joins the war, killing any hopes the 4077th has about a speedy resolution and homecoming.
Plastic Dreamworld: Matthew goes on a spending spree after inheriting a large sum of money from his grandfather.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 2: Jonathan sets out to restore Deke's faith in himself with the help of a young gymnast.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Divine Madness: Mr Crook, convinced he is King Arthur, is in danger of being declared incompetent by his son. Can Jonathan work a reconciliation?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Inner Limits: A young man learns that the brother he once considered institutionalised has a brilliant mind within a paralysed body.
It's A Dog's Life: Thinking Jonathan has turned into a dog, Mark embarks alone to unite a young runaway with his family.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Haunted House: Although many in Walnut Grove think he's a crazy man who lives in a haunted house, Laura befriends a mysterious hermit and soon unearths the truth behind his reclusive life.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Spring Dance: Caroline is puzzled when her plan to make Isaiah Edwards jealous fails.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Remember Me: Part 1: Widow Julia Sanderson is unafraid when Doc Baker diagnoses her with a terminal illness, but she knows she must help her three children cope with the news. Part 1 of 2.
The Long Night: With Grandma in the hospital, Grandpa feels alone and unneeded. But will an unlikely, blossoming friendship be enough to lift his spirits?
The Hiding Place: The Baldwins' niece Hilary arrives on Walton's Mountain, seeking a hiding place from the horrors of war breaking out in her native Austria.
The Final Frontier: There are some strange goings-on in the stratosphere over Ballykissangel. Niamh faces her feelings for Sean.
And Is There Honey Still for Tea?: Tom advertises for a replacement doctor; Linda and Len face a challenging time with their new foster child.
When Lady Cora's pregnant maid is rushed to the Thrift with labour pains, no one suspects she will lead them into the criminal underworld and bring Eleanor's father back to surgery.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Fascist Beast: Rumpole defends a retired army Captain who faces serious charges under the Race Relations Act. Meanwhile, Rumpole mentors a new pupil.
The Interview: The men are asked by interviewer Clete Roberts about their feelings on the war and its effects on them, and their futures for a documentary.
Bug Out - Part 1: When a rumour for a camp relocation turns out to be real, Hawkeye, Margaret and Radar volunteer to stay behind for a patient who cannot be moved.
Bug Out - Part 2: Hawkeye, Margaret 'Hot Lips' and Radar quickly learn after the unit departs that they're in the midst of the battlefront.
...the Likely Lads?: The Old Magic: Terry is disappointed that Bob has asked someone else to be his best man at the wedding but eventually agrees to go out for a meal with his friend.
...the Likely Lads?: Countdown: Mrs Chambers has grandiose plans for Thelma's wedding to Bob that leave the men feeling overwhelmed, so they decide to rebel.
...Likely Lads?: Boys' Night In: Terry is horrified when Bob refuses to go on a stag night and insists on a quiet night in instead.
Trap: Steed and the team find themselves in danger as a prolific criminal looks to exact their revenge on them.
